findings.summary: Extend the import pipeline beyond Bilibili to YouTube, direct video URLs, and local files. Wire up yt-dlp dependency for platform-agnostic video download and subtitle extraction.

new_files.purpose: URL pattern matching for Bilibili, YouTube, Twitter/X, TikTok, direct MP4 links. Returns Platform enum + normalized metadata stubs. bilinote reference: video.ts resolveVideoSource().
Platform-agnostic import orchestrator. Routes to B站 API, yt-dlp, or direct HTTP download based on platform. Produces uniform VideoMetadata + subtitle_text.
Package init.

FastAPI router: POST /api/import/url (SSE-progress import), POST /api/import/local (local file upload), GET /api/import/jobs/{jobId} (job status), DELETE /api/import/jobs/{jobId} (cancel). All endpoints stream PipelineProgress via SSE.
Check yt-dlp and ffmpeg versions at startup. Return health status. Show install instructions in frontend when missing.
Client-side SSE reader, platform auto-detection badge, progress step visualization, cancel button logic. Currently inlined in multi-platform.html; extract for testability.

modified_files.change: Add SourceKind.YOUTUBE, SourceKind.TWITTER, SourceKind.TIKTOK, SourceKind.LOCAL_FILE. Add Stage.DOWNLOADING stage between QUEUED and EXTRACTING for non-Bilibili sources.
modified_files.risk: Low. Enum additions are backward-compatible. Existing callers use existing enum members.

modified_files.change: Generalize run() to accept optional platform: Platform param. When platform != BILIBILI, delegate metadata fetch to multi_platform.importer instead of bilibili_client. ASR fallback path unchanged.
modified_files.risk: Medium. run() is the single import entry point. Must preserve exact behavior for BILIBILI path. Covered by existing test_smoke.py which exercises BILIBILI path; add YouTube fixture.

modified_files.change: Add try/except block to register routers.multi_platform router. Add dependency_checker startup hook.
modified_files.risk: Low. Standard registration pattern used by 10+ existing routers.

modified_files.change: Add platform field (default 'bilibili') to KB JSON schema. Schema migration: add default for existing entries.
modified_files.risk: Low. New field with default; existing entries unaffected.

modified_files.change: Replace inlined SSE logic with multi-platform.js import. Add YouTube/TikTok/Twitter platform badges. Add cancel button wired to DELETE /api/import/jobs/{jobId}.
modified_files.risk: Medium. User-facing UI. Must preserve existing Bilibili import flow. Rollback: revert to inlined version.

new_endpoints: POST /api/import/url - SSE streaming import: body={url, generate_summary, classify}, returns SSE with PipelineProgress events
POST /api/import/local - Multipart file upload import: form={file, generate_summary, classify}, returns SSE with PipelineProgress events
GET /api/import/jobs/{jobId} - Job status query (for polling fallback if SSE disconnected)
DELETE /api/import/jobs/{jobId} - Cancel running job
GET /api/dependencies/status - Returns {yt_dlp: version|missing, ffmpeg: version|missing, faster_whisper: installed|missing}
ui_components: Platform auto-detect badge (B站 pink / YouTube red / TikTok black / X blue / Direct gray) appearing beside URL input
5-step progress stepper: Download -> Extract -> Transcribe -> Summarize -> Save, with animated checkmarks
Cancel button (red, appears during import, wired to DELETE job endpoint with confirmation dialog)
Supported-platform hint chips (B站, YouTube, TikTok, X/Twitter, direct MP4/MKV) below URL input
Dependency health panel on tools.html showing yt-dlp/ffmpeg versions with one-click install instructions

dependencies.name: yt-dlp
dependencies.purpose: Video download from 1800+ platforms. Already a subprocess dependency in frame_extractor.py:67. New: used as primary download engine for non-Bilibili imports.

dependencies.name: ffmpeg
dependencies.purpose: Audio extraction and format conversion. Already a subprocess dependency in asr_service.py:57 (ffprobe). New: used for audio extraction from downloaded video files.

dependencies.name: python-multipart
dependencies.purpose: Multipart form parsing for local file upload endpoint. Standard FastAPI dependency.

dependencies.name: sse-starlette
dependencies.purpose: Server-Sent Events for progress streaming. Alternative: use StreamingResponse natively (FastAPI built-in).

findings.risk: MEDIUM. yt-dlp subprocess management: must enforce --no-playlist, timeouts (120s default), and temp file cleanup. Platform-specific subtitle format parsing (YouTube VTT vs Bilibili JSON) requires format-specific parsers. SSE connection lifecycle: client disconnect must cancel the job.
findings.rollback: Remove routers/multi_platform.py registration from main.py. Revert multi-platform.html to inlined SSE logic. UnifiedPipeline.run() preserves exact BILIBILI path behavior; rollback is a single-line import removal.

findings.phase: Phase 2: Local Video Upload + Unified Pipeline Integration
findings.summary: Enable drag-and-drop local video file import. Complete the unified pipeline integration so ALL import paths (URL, local, favorites sync, manual save) go through UnifiedPipeline.run(). Replace the 3 remaining ad-hoc assembly paths.

new_files.purpose: Local video file processor. Extracts audio via ffmpeg, runs faster-whisper ASR, produces TextSources. No B站 API dependency. bilinote reference: server.ts processLocalVideoJob().
Package init.
FastAPI router: POST /api/upload/video (multipart file upload, SSE progress). GET /api/upload/formats (supported video formats: mp4, mkv, avi, mov, webm, flv, wmv).

modified_files.change: Add local_file_path: Optional[str] parameter to run(). When provided, skip platform metadata fetch and delegate audio extraction + ASR to local_video.processor. TextSources assembly unchanged.
modified_files.risk: Low. New optional parameter, existing callers unaffected.

modified_files.change: Replace 70-line inline text assembly with: pipeline = UnifiedPipeline(); result = await pipeline.run(bvid=bvid, source_kind=SourceKind.MANUAL_SAVE). This is the P1 consolidation documented in unified_pipeline.py lines 579-599.
modified_files.risk: MEDIUM. This is the most impactful consolidation. api_rag_save is the primary manual-save entry point. Must preserve: danmaku inclusion, comment inclusion, metadata fallback, KB save, FTS5 index, ChromaDB index, classifier. Covered by test_smoke.py test_rag_save_flow.

modified_files.change: Replace both ad-hoc assembly paths with UnifiedPipeline.run_batch() for _do_sync and UnifiedPipeline.run() for api_favorites_import. This is the P2/P3 consolidation.
modified_files.risk: MEDIUM. Batch sync is the heaviest import path (100+ videos). Must preserve semaphore concurrency control (max_concurrent=3) and skip_existing logic. Covered by integration test.

modified_files.change: Register routers/upload.py router.
modified_files.risk: Low.

modified_files.change: Add import_jobs table: id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, bvid TEXT, platform TEXT, status TEXT, progress INTEGER, message TEXT, error TEXT, created_at TEXT, updated_at TEXT, result_json TEXT. Migration: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS.
modified_files.risk: Low. New table, no impact on existing schema.

modified_files.lines_ref: Lines 1-200 (entire page currently exists as placeholder)
modified_files.change: Build full upload UI: drag-and-drop zone with file type validation, format support table, ASR model selector (tiny/base/small/medium), progress stepper, result preview with save-to-KB button.
modified_files.risk: Medium. New UI from scratch but isolated to single page.

new_endpoints: POST /api/upload/video - Multipart upload + SSE: form={file, asr_model, generate_summary}, returns SSE PipelineProgress
GET /api/upload/formats - Returns {supported: ['mp4','mkv','avi','mov','webm','flv','wmv'], max_size_mb: 2048}
ui_components: Drag-and-drop zone (dashed border, 400px tall, accepts video/* MIME types)
File info card post-drop: filename, size, duration (if ffprobe available), format badge
ASR model selector dropdown (tiny/base/small/medium) with size estimate
Progress stepper: Upload -> Extract Audio -> Transcribe -> Summarize -> Save
Result preview card with text excerpt, save-to-KB button, classification tags

dependencies.name: python-multipart
dependencies.purpose: Multipart file upload parsing (shared with Phase 1).

dependencies.name: aiofiles
dependencies.purpose: Async file I/O for streaming large uploads to temp storage without blocking event loop.

findings.risk: MEDIUM-HIGH. Local video upload brings large file handling (up to 2GB), temporary storage management, and ffmpeg subprocess reliability concerns. Must enforce: temp file cleanup on cancel/failure, upload size limit (2GB), format whitelist. ASR on local videos can take 5-30 minutes for long content; SSE connection must stay alive.
findings.rollback: Remove upload.py router registration. upload.html is a new page; reverting to placeholder is trivial. UnifiedPipeline changes are additive (new optional param). kb.py and favorites.py refactors are the riskiest; rollback by reverting to pre-refactor inline assembly (lines preserved in unified_pipeline.py docstrings).

findings.phase: Phase 3: Mindmap v2 - Interactive, Exportable, Editable
findings.summary: Replace the current markmap autoloader (CDN-dependent, no editing, SVG export only) with a self-hosted interactive mindmap renderer. Add node editing, color themes, PDF export, and Obsidian Canvas export.

new_files.purpose: Self-contained interactive mindmap renderer using D3.js (v7, ~30KB min). Features: drag-to-rearrange nodes, double-click to edit, collapsible branches, 3 color themes (light/dark/print), zoom/pan, node search, keyboard shortcuts. Replaces CDN markmap-autoloader dependency.
Mindmap-specific styles: node shapes (rounded rect, pill, circle), edge curves (cubic bezier, step, straight), theme variables (3 themes), animation keyframes, toolbar styling.

Server-side mindmap export: SVG (raw XML with embedded CSS), PNG (via CairoSVG or headless browser), PDF (via WeasyPrint or similar), Obsidian Canvas JSON format. Accepts mindmap JSON tree from frontend.
Package init.
FastAPI router: POST /api/mindmap/export/svg, POST /api/mindmap/export/png, POST /api/mindmap/export/pdf, POST /api/mindmap/export/canvas (Obsidian Canvas JSON). All accept mindmap JSON tree body.

modified_files.change: Replace entire mindmap rendering block. renderMindmap() now calls mindmap-v2.js instead of loading CDN markmap-autoloader. New toolbar: theme switcher, node search, expand-all/collapse-all, export dropdown (SVG/PNG/PDF/Canvas). Remove 15s fallback timer and MutationObserver polling (lines 383-431).
modified_files.risk: MEDIUM. Core user-facing feature. Must preserve: mindmap mode selector in mode chips (line 77), toggle between map/outline views, export buttons (SVG, Obsidian). D3.js is self-hosted (bundled in repo) removing CDN dependency.

modified_files.lines_ref: Lines 152-196 (mindmap JSON schema) and lines 425-460 (mindmap prompt template) and lines 1287-1310 (structured output mindmap prompt)
modified_files.change: Enhance mindmap JSON schema: add node colors, icons, priority (1-5), and notes fields. Upgrade prompt template to request richer structure (max depth 4, min branches 3). Add timestamp-linked nodes option (each node can reference a video timestamp).
modified_files.risk: Low. Schema additions are backward-compatible (new optional fields). Existing mindmap prompts still work.

modified_files.change: Update mindmap prompt to match enhanced schema. Request icon hints (emoji) per node and priority levels.
modified_files.risk: Low.

new_endpoints: POST /api/mindmap/export/svg - Body: {tree: MindMapNode, theme: 'light'|'dark'|'print', width: int, height: int}. Returns SVG XML.
POST /api/mindmap/export/png - Same body. Returns PNG image.
POST /api/mindmap/export/pdf - Same body + page_size: 'A4'|'A3'|'letter'. Returns PDF.
POST /api/mindmap/export/canvas - Body: {tree, vault_path}. Generates Obsidian Canvas .canvas JSON. Returns downloadable file.
ui_components: Interactive D3.js force-directed tree layout with drag-to-reposition nodes
Toolbar: theme switcher (sun/moon/print icons), search box (highlight matching nodes), expand-all/collapse-all toggles, zoom-in/out/reset buttons
Export dropdown button: SVG, PNG (high-res), PDF (A4), Obsidian Canvas (.canvas JSON)
Node context menu (right-click): Edit text, Add child, Delete node, Change color, Set priority (1-5 stars)
Keyboard shortcuts: Ctrl+Z undo, Ctrl+Y redo, Delete remove node, Enter add sibling, Tab add child, Ctrl+F search
Mini-map in bottom-right corner for large mindmaps

dependencies.name: d3
dependencies.purpose: Interactive force-directed graph for mindmap editing. Self-hosted (bundled in frontend/js/vendor/), no CDN. ~30KB gzipped for the modules used (force, zoom, drag, selection).

dependencies.name: cairosvg
dependencies.purpose: SVG-to-PNG/PDF conversion. Pure Python, no system dependencies on Windows (uses cairocffi). Optional: can fall back to client-side canvas-based export.

dependencies.name: weasyprint
dependencies.purpose: PDF generation from SVG/HTML. Alternative: use client-side window.print() for PDF, no server dependency needed.

findings.risk: MEDIUM. D3.js has a learning curve and force-directed layouts can be janky on large trees (50+ nodes). Must implement: node culling for trees deeper than 4 levels, collision detection to prevent label overlap, debounced re-render on window resize. Self-hosting D3 removes CDN failure risk (current markmap loads from jsdelivr).
findings.rollback: Revert summary.html renderMindmap() to CDN markmap-autoloader version (lines 362-500 preserved in git). Remove mindmap-v2.js and mindmap-v2.css. Disable mindmap router registration in main.py. Prompt changes are additive; old prompts still produce valid mindmap markdown.

findings.summary: Build a comprehensive export system (PDF learning notes, HTML interactive report, ZIP bundle) and persistent job history with search, filtering, and re-import. Integrate jobs table from Phase 2 with a new history dashboard page.

new_files.purpose: Generate polished PDF learning notes with: cover page (title, B站 stats, QR code to video), TOC, summary section, transcript with timestamps, mindmap (embedded as vector SVG), comment analysis section, key terms glossary. Uses WeasyPrint or ReportLab.

Generate self-contained interactive HTML report: collapsible sections, embedded mindmap (D3.js inlined), timestamp-to-video links, dark/light theme toggle, search within transcript. Single-file, zero dependencies.
Package init.

Job history CRUD with SQLite persistence. Query jobs by status/date/platform/search. Auto-cleanup old jobs (configurable retention, default 30 days). bilinote reference: jobs.ts JobRecord + loadPersistedJobs().
Package init.

FastAPI router: GET /api/jobs (list with pagination, status filter, date range, search), GET /api/jobs/{jobId} (detail with result), DELETE /api/jobs/{jobId}, POST /api/jobs/{jobId}/retry (re-import from job record), DELETE /api/jobs/cleanup (remove old jobs).

Job history dashboard page. Table with columns: date, platform badge, title, status chip, progress bar, duration, actions (view result, retry, delete). Filters: platform, status, date range. Search: full-text over job title/message. Stats header: total jobs, success rate, avg duration.

modified_files.lines_ref: Lines 1-93 (entire file, currently only MD + DOCX export)
modified_files.change: Add POST /api/export/pdf endpoint (call pdf_exporter.generate_pdf), POST /api/export/html endpoint (call html_exporter.generate_html), POST /api/export/bundle endpoint (ZIP containing MD + DOCX + PDF + HTML + SRT). Enhance existing DOCX export to include mindmap section from Phase 3.
modified_files.risk: Low. Additive endpoints. Existing DOCX endpoint preserved.

modified_files.lines_ref: Lines 380-418 (end of run() method, around PipelineResult creation)
modified_files.change: After successful run(), persist a JobRecord to import_jobs table. Include: bvid, platform, title, status='done', progress=100, duration_ms, result summary (text_length, chunk_count, transcript_source).
modified_files.risk: Low. Additive persistence; job record is best-effort (failure does not block pipeline completion).

modified_files.change: Add import_jobs table if not already added in Phase 2. Add migration for existing history entries (backfill as one-off job records with status='done').
modified_files.risk: Low.

modified_files.change: Register routers/jobs.py router. Add job cleanup background task (runs daily, deletes jobs older than retention_days setting).
modified_files.risk: Low.

modified_files.change: Add 'Export PDF' and 'Export HTML' buttons next to existing 'Export DOCX' button. Wire to new export endpoints.
modified_files.risk: Low. UI addition.

new_endpoints: POST /api/export/pdf - Body: {bvid, title, author, summary, transcript, tags, mindmap_tree, chapters}. Returns PDF binary with Content-Disposition attachment.
POST /api/export/html - Body: same as PDF. Returns self-contained HTML file.
POST /api/export/bundle - Body: same. Returns ZIP containing {bvid}_summary.md, {bvid}_summary.docx, {bvid}_summary.pdf, {bvid}_summary.html, {bvid}_transcript.srt.
GET /api/jobs - Query: platform, status, search, limit, offset. Returns paginated job list.
GET /api/jobs/{jobId} - Returns full job record with result summary.
DELETE /api/jobs/{jobId} - Delete job record and optionally its KB entry.
POST /api/jobs/{jobId}/retry - Re-run the pipeline from the original job parameters.
DELETE /api/jobs/cleanup - Query: older_than_days. Deletes old job records.
ui_components: Jobs dashboard page (jobs.html): data table with sortable columns, filter chips for platform/status, date range picker, search bar
Job detail modal: stage timeline (vertical stepper), result preview (truncated text), action buttons (view in KB, re-import, delete)
Export format selector dropdown on summary page: MD, DOCX, PDF, HTML, ZIP bundle, with format-specific options (PDF page size, HTML theme)
Stats header on jobs page: total jobs, success rate %, avg duration, platform breakdown pie chart (simple CSS chart, no library)

dependencies.name: weasyprint
dependencies.purpose: PDF generation from HTML/CSS templates. Requires GTK3 on Linux (system dep); on Windows, can use wkhtmltopdf as lighter alternative.

dependencies.name: jinja2
dependencies.purpose: HTML template rendering for PDF and interactive HTML reports. Already a FastAPI transitive dependency.

findings.risk: MEDIUM. PDF generation is inherently fragile across platforms (WeasyPrint needs system libs on Linux, wkhtmltopdf needs separate install). Mitigation: make PDF export optional (greyed out with install instructions if deps missing). Job history is low-risk (new table, isolated feature). Bundle export may produce large ZIPs for long videos; enforce size limit (50MB).
findings.rollback: Remove jobs.py router registration. Remove exporters/ directory. Revert export.py to current state (MD + DOCX only). Job records in import_jobs table are non-critical; dropping the table has no impact on core functionality.

findings.summary: End-to-end integration tests covering all 5 import paths, mindmap rendering, all export formats, and job history lifecycle. UX polish: loading states, error boundaries, accessibility audit, performance optimization.

new_files.purpose: Pytest-asyncio integration tests using httpx.AsyncClient against a running FastAPI app. Covers: Bilibili URL import (mock B站 API), YouTube URL import (mock yt-dlp), local file upload (temp video fixture), favorites batch sync, job history CRUD, mindmap export (all 4 formats), DOCX/PDF/HTML/ZIP export, cancel mid-pipeline, error recovery (network timeout, invalid URL, missing deps). Uses test fixtures: sample_video.mp4 (5s silent video), mock_bilibili_api (httpx mock transport), mock_yt_dlp (subprocess mock).

Unit tests for mindmap tree operations: JSON schema validation, tree depth enforcement, node add/delete/reorder, SVG generation (valid XML), PNG conversion (non-empty bytes), Obsidian Canvas JSON (schema compliance).

Unit tests for all export formats: DOCX (valid .docx ZIP structure, CJK font embedding), PDF (non-empty, correct page count), HTML (valid HTML5, self-contained, no external resources), ZIP bundle (correct file listing, all formats present).

Test each pipeline stage in isolation: EXTRACTING (platform detection, subtitle fetch), TRANSCRIBING (ASR fallback, audio extraction), SUMMARIZING (prompt assembly, token budget), SAVING (KB JSON, FTS5, ChromaDB, classifier). Mock external dependencies.
Shared pytest fixtures: temp KB directory, test database, mock B站 API responses, mock LLM responses, sample video file generator, FastAPI TestClient factory.

modified_files.change: Add smoke tests for new endpoints: POST /api/import/url, POST /api/upload/video, POST /api/mindmap/export/svg, POST /api/export/pdf, GET /api/jobs. Each test verifies HTTP 200 + valid response shape. Add dependency health check test.
modified_files.risk: Low. Additive test cases.

modified_files.change: Add max_retries_per_video=2 to run_batch(). Add structured error collection (PipelineError list with per-bvid details). Add progress callback for batch-level progress (completed/total).
modified_files.risk: Low. Additive enhancements.

modified_files.change: Add loading skeleton (pulsing placeholder cards) while fetching. Add retry button with countdown on failure. Add estimated time remaining display during progress.
modified_files.risk: Low. UX polish.

modified_files.change: Add: formatDuration (already exists), formatFileSize, formatRelativeTime, debounce utility, escapeHtml (already exists). Add Toast types: success, error, warning, info, loading (with spinner). Add ErrorBoundary component for graceful JS error display.
modified_files.risk: Low. Utility additions.

modified_files.change: Add animation keyframes: skeleton-pulse, fade-in, slide-up, spin. Add .skeleton class for loading placeholders. Add .error-boundary class for JS error fallback UI. Add print media query (@media print) for clean PDF/print output.
modified_files.risk: Low. CSS additions.

new_endpoints: GET /api/health/full - Comprehensive health check: database, B站 API, yt-dlp, ffmpeg, faster-whisper, ChromaDB, disk space, memory usage. Returns detailed status per component.
None. Phase 5 is primarily testing and polish; no new functional endpoints beyond those defined in Phases 1-4.
ui_components: Loading skeleton cards (animated placeholder with shimmer effect) replacing spinner overlay
Error boundary component: catches JS errors, shows friendly message with retry and report buttons
Toast notification system upgrade: stacked toasts (max 3), auto-dismiss with progress bar, action buttons (undo, retry)
Empty states for all pages: illustrated placeholder with CTA button (e.g., 'No jobs yet. Import your first video!')
Keyboard shortcut help modal (? key): lists all shortcuts across pages
Accessibility: ARIA labels on all interactive elements, focus trapping in modals, skip-to-content link, screen reader announcements for async updates (aria-live regions)
Performance: lazy-load D3.js only on summary page with mindmap mode, debounced search inputs, virtual scrolling for job history list (1000+ entries)

dependencies.name: pytest-asyncio
dependencies.purpose: Async test support for FastAPI endpoints.

dependencies.name: httpx
dependencies.purpose: HTTP client for integration tests (already a production dependency).

dependencies.name: pytest-cov
dependencies.purpose: Test coverage reporting. Target: 80%+ line coverage on new code.

dependencies.name: respx
dependencies.purpose: HTTPX mock transport for mocking B站 API and LLM API calls in tests.

findings.risk: LOW. Testing and polish phase is inherently low-risk for production code. Integration tests may surface hidden bugs in Phases 1-4 code; these are caught before release. Performance optimization (D3 lazy loading, virtual scrolling) is additive and can be reverted individually.
findings.rollback: No production code changes that require rollback. Test files are standalone. Polish changes (CSS animations, toast upgrades) are cosmetic and backward-compatible.
